essence: Develops plans, procedures, and coordination frameworks to prepare communities and organisations for major incidents and emergencies.

identity.title: Emergency Planning Officer
identity.summary: Creates emergency response plans, coordinates multi‑agency preparedness, runs training exercises, and supports resilience during major incidents.

salary.notes: Senior resilience officers, major‑incident coordinators, and regional leads can exceed £50k-£60k+.

qualifications: Training in emergency planning, civil protection, or resilience
Knowledge of risk assessment and major‑incident frameworks
Understanding of multi‑agency coordination (police, fire, NHS, local authorities)
Ability to run exercises, simulations, and training sessions

lifestyle.description: Emergency planning officers work with councils, emergency services, and organisations to prepare for major incidents such as floods, fires, pandemics, and infrastructure failures. The role involves risk assessments, writing response plans, running exercises, and supporting real‑time incident coordination when required. It suits individuals who enjoy strategic thinking, public safety, and multi‑agency collaboration.

habits: Maintains up‑to‑date emergency plans and procedures
Coordinates with multiple agencies and stakeholders
